task_text: We're about to kick off Q3 planning. Write a one-page risk summary that identifies the top risks for the Q3 roadmap and maps each risk to the current project situation. Save it as q3_risk_summary.txt.

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

content.text: I've identified the top risks from the roadmap and current status. Now I'll draft your one-page Q3 risk summary.

input.content:
Project Helios — Q3 Risk Summary
Date: 2026-07-10
Prepared for: Q3 Planning Kickoff
Classification: INTERNAL

EXECUTIVE OVERVIEW
Q3 2026 is critical for Helios with five major phases: System Integration (July), Meridian Product Demo (July 25), UAT (Aug 17-28), Performance Hardening (Aug-Sep), and Launch Prep (Target: Oct 1). Overall status is ON TRACK with minor risks, but four active high-scoring risks directly threaten the July 25 demo and Oct 1 launch. Any UAT slippage >2 weeks triggers Section 7.1 penalty review per roadmap draft.

TOP RISKS MAPPED TO CURRENT PROJECT SITUATION

1. R-01: Meridian Source Data Quality Issues | Score 9 (High/High) - TOP RISK
   - Risk Register Status: Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
   - Current Situation: Weekly Status (Week of 2026-07-06) lists Open Blocker #1: Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs MM/DD/YYYY). Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay 2-3 days. Data ingestion module only 85% complete.
   - Q3 Impact: Directly threatens Q3 Roadmap Priority #1 (Finalize ingestion by 2026-07-18) and Priority #2 Demo (Live data ingestion using Meridian sample data per contract Sec 9.3). If unresolved, integration test sign-off (July 25) will slip.
   - Mitigation Owner: Amir Hassan

2. R-02: GeoSync License Not Renewed Before Demo | Score 6 (Medium/High)
   - Risk Register Status: Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
   - Current Situation: Status Update Blocker #2: Third-party mapping library GeoSync v2.1 license renewal pending. Legal reviewing. This is a gating item for integration testing (currently 40% complete).
   - Q3 Impact: Blocks Priority #1 Integration Test Sign-off (target July 25) and Priority #2 Demo. License lapse would require code rework or feature removal.
   - Mitigation Owner: Raj Patel

3. R-03: Dashboard Performance on Large Datasets | Score 6 (Medium/High)
   - Risk Register Status: Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
   - Current Situation: Client dashboard only 60% complete vs API gateway 100%. Dashboard v1 target July 22. No performance baselines yet.
   - Q3 Impact: Performance Hardening phase (Aug-Sep) requires <2 sec load for 100k records and 500k records/min ingestion throughput plus external penetration test. If architecture issues exist, they will surface late, impacting UAT and Launch Prep in September.
   - Mitigation Owner: Derek Okafor

4. R-04: UAT Timeline Slippage | Score 6 (High/Medium)
   - Risk Register Status: Active — 1-week extension already consumed
   - Current Situation: Roadmap allocates Aug 17-28 for Meridian UAT with only 2 sprints budget for feedback. With ingestion and dashboard behind, integration testing (40% complete) has limited buffer before UAT environment deploy.
   - Q3 Impact: Highest business impact. Roadmap explicitly notes: UAT slippage >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review. Current ON TRACK status leaves no spare contingency.
   - Mitigation Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

Secondary Watch Items:
- R-05 Key Person Dependency (Derek Okafor) — Low likelihood but High impact; no backup tech lead identified while dashboard performance risk remains open.
- R-06 Meridian IT Access Delays — Awaiting infrastructure diagram; could compound R-01.

RECOMMENDATIONS FOR Q3 PLAN
1. Escalate R-01 data format clarification with Meridian IT daily; define fallback parsing logic.
2. Accelerate R-02 legal review to complete before July 14 deadline; prepare contingency to remove GeoSync dependency for demo.
3. Start HEL-247 early benchmarking in Sprint 14 despite 60% completion to de-risk hardening phase.
4. Protect UAT buffer: lock integration sign-off criteria now and freeze optional scope (scheduled ingestion jobs, email alerting, mapping editor) under change control.

Conclusion: The July 25 demo is the immediate risk convergence point for R-01 and R-02. UAT timeline protection must be Q3 planning priority.

content.text:
Q3 risk summary completed and saved as **q3_risk_summary.txt**.

The summary was created using:
- **q3_roadmap_draft.txt** (July 25 demo, UAT Aug 17-28, Oct 1 launch target, Section 7.1 penalty clause)
- **risk_register.txt** (9 risks scored, 4 active high scores)
- **project_helios_status.txt** (85% ingestion, 60% dashboard, 40% integration testing, 2 active blockers)

Top 4 risks mapped:
1. **R-01 Data Quality (Score 9)** - Active ISO 8601 vs MM/DD/YYYY blocker, threatens ingestion completion (July 18) and live demo
2. **R-02 GeoSync License (Score 6)** - Legal review due July 14, threatens integration sign-off
3. **R-03 Dashboard Performance (Score 6)** - HEL-247 not started, 60% dashboard completion vs <2sec target
4. **R-04 UAT Slippage (Score 6)** - 1-week extension already consumed, >2 week slip triggers Q4 launch delay
